title: Cleretum schlechteri

The species Cleretum schlechteri is further categorized as a flowering plant, specifically as a succulent perennial. This classification is based on its growth habits, leaf structure, and reproductive features. As a member of the Aizoaceae family, Cleretum schlechteri shares characteristics with other plants in this group.

intro: Cleretum schlechteri, also known as the Klein Karoo annual or Schlechter's annual, is a succulent plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used in South African folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, providing potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Bioactive constituents of Cleretum schlechteri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and capsules, with the most common herbal preparation being a standardized extract of the plant's flowers and leaves.
